<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README START -->
# theory_checks

This folder contains the public checker for the released Paper B Lean
bridge.

## What It Checks

`check_paper_b_theory.py` scans `../3.lean/KO7Benchmark/PaperB/*.lean`
and verifies three things:

- every expected theorem name for the released bridge is present;
- no non-comment line contains forbidden tokens such as `sorry`,
  `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`;
- no import pulls in the private-only `KO7Lib` stack.

With `--build`, it also runs:

```powershell
lake build KO7Benchmark.PaperB
```

against the Lean root passed with `--root`.

## Why This Lives In `7.scoring`

The main scoring scripts consume only the public answer key and the
released CSVs. This checker is separate because it does not derive verdict
columns. Instead, it gives reviewers a direct way to verify that the
theorem-facing `PaperB` lane shipped in `3.lean/` still matches the
appendix claims and remains free of private-stack imports or placeholder
proofs.

## Files

- `check_paper_b_theory.py`: theorem-lane checker script.
- `paper_b_theory_check_report.json`: machine-readable report written by
  the checker.
- `paper_b_theory_check_report.md`: reviewer-facing markdown summary of
  the same run.

## Typical Command

From the repository root:

```powershell
python .\7.scoring\theory_checks\check_paper_b_theory.py --root .\3.lean --build
```

The checker writes its reports back into this folder, so the JSON and
Markdown files are always the latest local audit trail.
